topic: Overhaul the hands-on lab: Cloud Run runs the webapp, Oracle 26ai DB runs on a GCE VM as a container, Cloud Build deploys changes, and a private VPC keeps the DB non-public
libraries_researched: Google Compute Engine (containers on VMs, Container-Optimized OS, cloud-init)
Cloud Run (Direct VPC egress, Serverless VPC Access connectors, Jobs)
key_findings: GCE can run a container directly, but create-with-container is deprecated; use cloud-init/startup-script docker run
App already supports a remote DB via DATABASE_HOST/PORT/SERVICE_NAME — no code change to point Cloud Run at a remote Oracle
spanner-mc cloudbuild (build->push->migrate->run deploy --vpc-connector) is a near-verbatim template
Migrating a PRIVATE-IP Oracle DB from Cloud Build needs a private pool, a Cloud Run Job w/ VPC, or VM-side migration — default pool can't reach it
Direct VPC egress is the recommended Cloud Run->VPC method over the connector spanner-mc used
Main risk is lab complexity vs the current beginner-friendly single-VM lab

decisions.lab_packaging: Two separate labs: rename current single-VM lab as GCE-only; author a brand-new Cloud Run + GCE-DB + Cloud Build + VPC lab module that follows it
decisions.migrations: Cloud Build private pool peered to VPC (scopes DB/VPC privileges to the build); fallback = Cloud Run Job with Direct VPC egress
decisions.db_vm_os: Container-Optimized OS with persistent disk mounted into the Oracle container via cloud-init
